title: My Year of Words: Grade 3 Capstone Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The people who live with you and love you.
rounds.answer: family
rounds.explanation: family: The people who live with you and love you.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The third month of the year, when heavy rains often arrive.
rounds.answer: March
rounds.explanation: March: The third month of the year, when heavy rains often arrive.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Nice and helpful to other people.
rounds.answer: kind
rounds.explanation: kind: Nice and helpful to other people.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A mother or a father who looks after you.
rounds.answer: parent

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The fourth month of the year, when flowers bloom in many places.
rounds.answer: april

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Happy to talk to people and play with them.
rounds.answer: friendly

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My family has my mum, my dad, my sister, and me.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The author wrote a funny book about a talking camel.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The rains often come in March.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence compares two months correctly?
choices: March was more wetter than April.
March was most wet than April.
March was the wettest than April.
rounds.answer: March was wetter than April.
rounds.explanation: Short adjectives add -er to compare two things, and the word more is not used as well.

rounds.prompt: Which word names a person who works on a boat or ship at sea?
rounds.answer: sailor
rounds.explanation: A sailor works at sea, while an officer keeps people safe on land and a library holds books.

rounds.prompt: Choose the best joining word: We needed the right size, ______ we measured the long table.
rounds.answer: so
rounds.explanation: Measuring the table is the result of needing the right size, and so points to a result.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What did Teacher Yasmin put on every desk, and what did she tell the class to choose?
choices: She put one large green folder on every desk, and she told them to choose the work that shows how far they have travelled.
In January Amal could hardly write one paragraph, and now she writes a whole page, so her writing has grown a lot.
The last verse says the nine doors gave the speaker one growing Year 3 voice, ready to walk onward into Grade 4.
rounds.answer: She put one large green folder on every desk, and she told them to choose the work that shows how far they have travelled.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She put one large green folder on every desk, and she told them to choose the work that shows how far they have travelled.

rounds.prompt: The Showcase brief lists four parts. What must you do in Part 3?
choices: In January Amal could hardly write one paragraph, and now she writes a whole page, so her writing has grown a lot.
The table is two metres and forty centimetres long, and six booklets will fit on it.
You speak for about two minutes beside your display table, point at your pictures and answer two questions.
rounds.answer: You speak for about two minutes beside your display table, point at your pictures and answer two questions.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: You speak for about two minutes beside your display table, point at your pictures and answer two questions.

rounds.prompt: How long is the table Maya measured, and how many booklets will fit on it?
choices: Because every parent has answered the invitation, so she knows exactly how many visitors are coming.
The table is two metres and forty centimetres long, and six booklets will fit on it.
She will read a long chapter book by herself, and she will offer to help a new student on the first day.
rounds.answer: The table is two metres and forty centimetres long, and six booklets will fit on it.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The table is two metres and forty centimetres long, and six booklets will fit on it.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Choosing My Six Pages
rounds.target: I chose my ______ page because it shows ______.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 — Review Words in Real Sentences
rounds.target: Our ______ climate suits an outdoor showcase.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — Answering Listeners' Questions
rounds.target: That is a good question. The reason is ______.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: My sister is resting at the hospital.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The kind girl shared her lunch with a new pupil.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Today the weather is sunny and warm.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'doctor' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who helps sick people feel better.
The people who live with you and love you.
A person you like and spend time with.
rounds.answer: A person who helps sick people feel better.
rounds.explanation: doctor: A person who helps sick people feel better.

rounds.prompt: What does 'honest' mean in this unit?
choices: The people who live with you and love you.
A person you like and spend time with.
Always telling the truth, even when it is hard.
rounds.answer: Always telling the truth, even when it is hard.
rounds.explanation: honest: Always telling the truth, even when it is hard.

rounds.prompt: What does 'friend' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who writes books or stories.
A person you like and spend time with.
The people who live with you and love you.
rounds.answer: A person you like and spend time with.
rounds.explanation: friend: A person you like and spend time with.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'listen'?
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: listen is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'sunshine'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: sunshine is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'graduate'?
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: graduate is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A person you like and spend time with.
A person who writes books or stories.
The first month of the year.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: A person who helps sick people feel better.
A place where doctors and nurses care for people who are sick or hurt.
To say kindly that you will give or do something.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Always telling the truth, even when it is hard.
What the sky and air are like today.
A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which question is written in the correct order?
choices: Where you did find that fact in the library?
Where did you find that fact in the library?
Where did you found that fact in the library?
Where find you did that fact in the library?
rounds.answer: Where did you find that fact in the library?
rounds.explanation: The helper did comes before the subject you, and the main verb stays plain: find.

rounds.prompt: The Year 3 Showcase brief has four parts. How many pages must your booklet hold?
rounds.answer: Six pages.
rounds.explanation: Part 1 of the brief tells you to pick six pages from at least four different units.

rounds.prompt: In "Amal's Year of Words", which page did Amal decide to keep for her showcase?
rounds.answer: Her kindness jar page.
rounds.explanation: After reading her honest sentence again, Amal decided to keep the kindness jar page.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word names the usual weather in a place over a long time?
rounds.answer: climate
rounds.explanation: Climate is the usual weather over many years, while weather is what the sky is like today.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the past simple correctly?
choices: Last March we built a reading wall.
Last March we builded a reading wall.
Last March we build a reading wall.
Last March we did built a reading wall.
rounds.answer: Last March we built a reading wall.
rounds.explanation: Build is irregular, so its past form is built, and the helper did is not needed here.

rounds.prompt: Which meaning belongs to the word graduate?
choices: To make something by putting parts together.
To use your ears to hear carefully.
To talk about something with other people.
To finish school or a class.
rounds.answer: To finish school or a class.
rounds.explanation: Amal's class will graduate from Year 3 at the end of term, which means they finish it.
